info.name: Pismo cards collection
info.description:
This collection allows you to test a basic use case for creating and managing cards using the Pismo endpoints. This collection assumes you already have some basic data in your organization such as a program configured with due dates.

For more information, visit [the tutorial](https://developers.pismo.io/pismo-docs/docs/sample-project) on the Pismo Developers Portal.

Before you begin:

- Work with your Pismo representative to set up your organization and at least one program configured with due dates. See Core objects for more information and getting started. Pismo Control Center allows you to do a lot of this setup through a convenient UI.
    
- Make sure you have the Pismo sandbox environment as the current environment and have updated the environment variables as detailed in the Postman collections guide.

item.name: Platform
Authenticate

request.description: Login your server to retrieve an access token. Further calls to Pismo's server will require an Authorization header.

Get an access token for OpenID authentication

NOTE: Many of the endpoints in the Pismo API require you to pass a JWT that you generated which includes a Pismo account ID (`uid` or `account_id`) or an account ID from an external system used in place of the Pismo account ID (`external_account_id`). Further calls to Pismo endpoints will require this token in the Authorization header. For more information, see [Endpoints that require an account-specific token](ref:endpoints-that-require-an-account-specific-token) and [Endpoints that require an external ID-specific token](ref:endpoints-that-require-an-external-id-token).

item.description: You can use either a Basic access token or an OpenID access token to authenticate to the Pismo platform. Work with your Pismo representative to gain access to a sandbox environment with an associated organization.

item.description: List or create programs for a given organization. For more information about programs, refer to [Core objects](https://developers.pismo.io/pismo-docs/docs/setup-overview#program).

item.name: Acquisitions

request.description: This endpoint creates a new application form. If the `submit` property is set to `false`, the application is not sent for processing and can be further PATCH'd with incremental changes. On the other hand if the `submit` flag is set to `true`, no further updates can be made, and the form is sent out for processing. The result of processing an acquisition form is the creation of an account and its associated structures.

header.value: default-src 'none'; script-src 'self'; connect-src 'self'; img-src 'self'; style-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'self'; form-action 'self';

item.description: An acquisition represents a new application form. The result of processing an acquisition form is the creation of an account and its associated structures. Once you create an account application, you can authenticate with that account association.
Use these endpoints to authentication, set up programs, and create an account. Once you have the account ID, you can use the endpoints in the Account specific folder.

item.description: Once you have an account ID, you can use it to request a token that’s associated with that account. You send your server key, server secret, and account ID to get an account-specific token that you use for the calls that require the account ID.

header.value: default-src 'none'; script-src 'self'; connect-src 'self'; img-src 'self'; style-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'self'; form-action 'self';

request.description:
There are 3 customer types you can create with this endpoint: a `company` customer, a `person` customer, or a person customer with less informational fields (`customer` object).

Each account contains at least one customer. You can add multiple customers to an account but only one can be the account owner. Other customers can have their own individual cards, but the balances on those cards must be shared with the account owner.

item.description: Create and manage accounts and its related data.

item.name: Payments
Make a transfer

request.description: Transfer funds between a source (from) and destination (to).

header.value: default-src 'none'; script-src 'self'; connect-src 'self'; img-src 'self'; style-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'self'; form-action 'self';

item.description:
Create a transfer operation between two accounts on the Pismo platform.

With this endpoint, you have the following additional options.

- Specify the `authentication` method and details.
    
- Set `pre_authorization` to `true` for operations that require additional confirmation and then call the [Confirm pre-authorized transfer](https://developers.pismo.io/pismo-docs/reference/post-transfers-confirm) endpoint.
    
- Set `validation_rules` in the `From` object to control the rules in this flow, such as disabling a rule, changing the response code and denial code, and forcing a rule.
    

Refer to the [Payment methods](https://developers.pismo.io/pismo-docs/docs/payment-methods) guide for additional information.

This endpoint generates a [Platform authorization created](https://developers.pismo.io/events/docs/platform-authorization-platform-authorization-1) event.

**Note:** This is a PCI endpoint, use the [https://gw-pci.pismolabs.io](https://gw-pci.pismolabs.io/) environment.

request.description: The `from` object is a `custom_info` structure, while the `to` is an `account`.

request.description: Use this endpoint to cancel a previously authorized operation. To perform a cancel operation, you can use either the `original_tracking_id` or the `authorization_id`, bu not both. The metadata value returned is the acquirer response if it's an external operation. If no processing codes are specified, we assume the same used for the original authorization.

item.name: Cards
Physical

request.description:
This endpoint allows for the creation of virtual as well as physical cards.

The payload object describes some aspects of the card to be created. The most important piece of information here is the card type. This will determie if the card will be a physical, embossed card or a virtual one. The possible values for this property are:
- `PLASTIC`: a physical card
- `RECURRING`: a virtual card used for recurring payments (monthly billing, subscriptions, etc)
- `TEMPORARY`: a virtual card valid for 24 hours
- `CARD_ON_FILE`: a default virtual card
Use this endpoint to retrieve non-sensitive information (e.g. card number, pin, etc) about a specific card.
This endpoint retrieves sensitive data from a card, that is stored in a separate secure infrastructure.

When a physical card is created it remains blocked for transactions until it is activated. This prevents unauthorized operations by persons other than the card holder, until its delvivery. Use this endpoint to activate them.
Use this endpoint to retrieve the Personal Identification Number (PIN) used for authenticating purchases with Pinpads.
Use this endpoint to update a card password.

item.description:
Create a virtual or physical (plastic/metal) card.

Refer to the [Cards management](https://developers.pismo.io/pismo-docs/docs/cards-overview-1) guide for more information about physical and virtual cards.

This endpoint generates a [Card created](https://developers.pismo.io/events/docs/cards-create-1) event. The card's PCI data is created asynchronously as is the Card created event. Any operation that tries to access a card before this event, such as update password or get PCI information, is going to return a **404, not found** error.

item.name: Virtual

request.description:
This endpoint allows for the creation of virtual as well as physical cards.

The payload object describes some aspects of the card to be created. The most important piece of information here is the card type. This will determie if the card will be a physical, embossed card or a virtual one. The possible values for this property are:
- `PLASTIC`: a physical card
- `RECURRING`: a virtual card used for recurring payments (monthly billing, subscriptions, etc)
- `TEMPORARY`: a virtual card valid for 24 hours
- `CARD_ON_FILE`: a default virtual card
Use this endpoint to retrieve non-sensitive information (e.g. card number, pin, etc) about a specific card.
This endpoint retrieves sensitive data from a card, that is stored in a separate secure infrastructure.

Use this endpoint to change the status of a card. This can effectively block the card to prevent further transactions, or revert it back to an active state.

The possible status for the card are:
- `NORMAL` the card is active and eligible for transactioning
- `BLOCKED` transactions for the card are temporarily blocked, until further status changes
- `PENDING` the card was created and are pending further processing
- `LOST` the card was reported lost and transactions are disabled
- `ROBBED` the card was reported robbed and transactions are disabled
- `EXPIRED` the card expired and transactions are disabled
- `DAMAGED` the card was reported damaged and transactions are disabled
- `FRAUD` the card has a fraud suspect and transactions are disabled
- `WARNING` the card has a general alert flag set and transactions are disabled
- `CANCELED` the card was cancelled and no further transactions are possible
- `CREATED` the card was created in the system
- `DELETED` the card was deleted and no further transactions will be possible

item.description:
Create a virtual or physical (plastic/metal) card.

Refer to the [Cards management](https://developers.pismo.io/pismo-docs/docs/cards-overview-1) guide for more information about physical and virtual cards.

This endpoint generates a [Card created](https://developers.pismo.io/events/docs/cards-create-1) event. The card's PCI data is created asynchronously as is the Card created event. Any operation that tries to access a card before this event, such as update password or get PCI information, is going to return a **404, not found** error.

query.description: Returns only cards with the desired status.

request.description: Use this endpoint to retrieve a list of cards from a particular account.

item.description: You need to make a card using the customer ID and account ID, either a physical card or a virtual card. A physical card means a plastic card, and a virtual card means an online card. You also activate a physical card and you can get PCI info on either a physical or virtual card.

item.name: Credit Card Purchase Simulator

body.raw:
{
    "card_id": "{{accounts.created.plasticCardId}}",
    "local_amount": 50,
    "local_currency": "986",
    "settlement_amount": 50,
    "entry_mode": "051",
    "merchant": {
        "name": "Woolley's Wool Store",
        "city": "New York",
        "country": "USA"
    },
    "installments": {
        "number": 3,
        "with_interest": false
    },
    "processing_code": "00",
    "mti": "0100",
    "account_type": "30",
    "invoice_address": "202, Nowhere St. - Somewhere City, KS 12345",
    "authorization_code": "56798A",
    "retrieval_reference_number": "{{$timestamp}}"
}

request.description: With this endpoint you can simulate branded authorizations like the ones performed with real networked acquirers.

item.description: These endpoints let you simulate a purchase using the newly-created credit card. First you simulate authorizing the purchase, then you simulate clearing the purchase.

item.name: Transactions

query.description: Find transactions belonging to a specific statement.
Find all transactions associated with a specific customer.
Filter a specific transactions by its type.
Find transactions related to the specified processing code.
Returns only credit transactions if set to `true`. When set to `false`, returns only debit transactions. Will return both types if omitted.
Filter on StatementPost field. This field indicates whether a transaction should be posted to the statement.
Retrieves all transactions associated with a specific authorization.
Filter transactions by their type authorization. Allowed 'NETWORK' or 'PLATFORM'
The lower bound date from which to filter transactions, formatted as a RFC3339 string.

The order of the result set. The results are always ordered by transaction date. This param indicates whether the order is ascending or descending.
Allowed:
'asc'
'desc'
The upper bound date to which to filter transactions, formatted as a RFC3339 string.
Number of latest transactions. Get the X latest transactions from the date specified in the event_date field. This filter overwrites page filters and doesn't allow any other filters with it.
Find all transactions associated with a specific Authorization tracking ID.
The amount of items in the paginated result set.
Page offset of transactions. Indicates which page to start with when returning the results. Defaults to 0.
Array of transaction IDs
Find all transactions associated with a specific user category
Find all transactions associated with a specific payment method ID

request.description:
List transactions without their metadata. If you provide at least one of the following parameters, the `eventDateStart` parameter is optional.

- `id`
    
- `authorizationId`
    
- `authorizationIds`
    
- `statementId`
    
- `authorizationTrackingId`
    
- `numberOfLatestTransactions`
    

If you don't provide any of the above parameters, you must include the `eventDateStart` parameter, and the maximum allowed query range is 90 days.
Use this endpoint to retrieve information about a specific transaction.

query.description: Find a specific transaction id
Filter all transactions related to the transaction_id informed (It won't work without the param transaction_id). It is useful to find installments or cancellations
Filter by authorization id
Filter by transaction type id
Filter by customer id
Filter by statement id
Filter by user category
Filter credit transactions when It is true, If false will find debit transactions. If not informed will find any type
Filter by payment method
Transaction event_date - begin date. The format is yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm(2008-09-15T15:53:00+05:00) (ISO 8601)
Transaction event_date - end date. The format is yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm(2008-09-15T15:53:00+05:00) (ISO 8601)

request.description: Use this endpoint to retrive the total number of transactions with several filter options.

item.description: Use these endpoints to simulate branded operations and retrieve transactions. Once you have simulated a few purchases, you can look at the transactions either by listing multiple transactions or getting information on one transaction using a transaction ID. You can also try filtering transactions with the Get transaction count endpoint. For example, you can filter by payment method or dates.

item.name: Statements

request.description: Use this endpoint to retrieve transactions, with several filters available.
Use this endpoint to retrieve transactions, with several filters available.
Use this endpoint to retrieve transactions, with several filters available.
Use this endpoint to retrieve transactions, with several filters available.

item.description: With those transactions available, you can now list some statements for the account ID. Statement balances are calculated based on transaction balances, and configurations for statements are set at the program or account level. Credit card balances and interest accruals are managed in cycles, or billing periods. Each statement corresponds to a single cycle, and when one cycle ends, another begins.

item.name: Events

query.description: The pagination number.
The maximum number of results per page.

request.description: This endpoint retrieves timeline events for a specific account. The account identifier must be sent in the header.

item.description: Use this endpoint to list past operations on the timeline, such as payments, transactions, or cancellations.

Use these endpoints to simulate account-based activities like payments, creating cards, making purchases, making statements, and viewing events on the timeline such as payments, transactions, or cancellations.
